sections.paragraph:
ἐπαινέω fut. ἐπαινέσω; 1 aor. ἐπῄνεσα; 1 fut. pass. ἐπαινεσθήσομαι LXX (s. next entry; Hom.+; ins [esp. in honorific decrees], pap, LXX, EpArist; JosAs 24:11; Philo, Joseph., Test12Patr; Tat.; Ath. 15, 2) to express one’s admiration for or approval of a pers., object, or event, praise τινά someone (Jos., Vi. 232 ἐμέ; 279; Tat.; Ath. 15, 2) 1 Cor 11:22a; IMg 12; ISm 5:2; MPol 4. ἑαυτὸν ἐ. praise oneself Hs 9, 22, 2. τὶ someth. (Aelian, VH 2, 12; Jos., Ant. 14, 293 τ. ἔργον; Ar. 13, 7; Just., D 2:5) 1 Cl 33:6. Abs. 1 Cor 11:22b, unless ὑμᾶς is to be supplied, cp. ibid. a. W. acc. and ὅτι foll. praise someone because or in that Lk 16:8 (s. the lit. on οἰκονόμος 1; JDerrett, NTS 7, ’60/61, 210 n. 3 ‘sanction’); 1 Cor 11:2. τοῦτο … οὐκ ἐπαινῶ ὅτι this is someth. I cannot praise (approve of), namely that 11:17 (for the sense ‘approve of’: Appian, Bell. Civ. 3, 14 §49; Ael. Aristid. 24, 22 K.=44 p. 831 D.; Aelian, Fgm. 235 p. 263, 17 τὸ θεῖον οὐκ ἐπῄνει τὰ ὑπὸ τ. βασιλέως πραττόμενα. Oft. Philostrat. [Schmid IV 294]; Jos., Ant. 14, 293. Other exx. in AFridrichsen, Horae Soederblomianae I/1, ’44, 28–32. On the contrast between what can be commanded and what cannot cp. SIG 22, 10–26.). Pass. ἡ γῆ τοῦ Ἰακὼβ ἐπαινουμένη παρὰ πᾶσαν τὴν γῆν praised above every land B 11:9 (quot. fr. an unknown prophet). Also in relig. usage: praise God (Philo) Ro 15:11 (Ps 116:1 v.l.); GJs 8:1.—DELG s.v. αἶνος. M-M. TW.

fut. fut. = fut.

aor. aor. = aorist

pass. pass. = passive (either of grammatical form or of passive experience); also used in reference to literary portion=passage

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

Hom Hom , VIII b.c.—List 5

ins Ins, ins = Inscription, Inschrift, inscription(s). Without a period, esp. in lists, as at the beginning of entries; the capitalized form is used in titles. In conjunction with literary works this abbr. refers to the title or description of contents.

esp. esp. = especially

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

EpArist EpArist = Epistle of Aristeas, II b.c.—List 5

JosAs JosAs = Joseph and Aseneth—Lists 2, 5

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Joseph Joseph This abbr. used when no specific texts are cited (s. Jos.); I a.d.—List 5

Test12Patr Test12Patr = Testaments of the Twelve Patriarchs, with interpolations II b.c.–III a.d.—List 2

Tat Tat , II a.d.—List 5

Ath. Ath. = Athenagoras, II a.d.—List 5

pers. pers. = person(s)

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

IMg IMg = Ignatius to the Magnesians—List 1

ISm ISm = Ignatius to the Smyrnaeans, I–II a.d.—List 1

MPol MPol = Martyrdom of Polycarp; after II a.d.—List 1

Hs Hs = Similitudes

someth. someth. = something

Aelian Aelian , II a.d.—List 5

Ar. Ar. = Aristides, apologist, II a.d.—List 5

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

1 Cl 1 Cl = 1 Clement—List 1

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

ibid. ibid. = ibidem (in the same book or passage)

acc. acc. = accusative

foll. foll. = followed, following

lit. lit. = literal(ly); literature (refererences to [scholarly] literature)

NTS NTS = New Testament Studies—List 6

Appian Appian , I–II a.d.—List 5

Ael. Ael. Aristid. = Aelius Aristides, II a.d.—List 5

Fgm. Fgm. = fragment, fragmentary

Philostrat Philostrat = Flavius Philostratus, the Sophist II–III a.d.—List 5

Schmid Schmid = WS., Der Atticismus in seinen Hauptvertretern—Lists 6

exx. exx. = examples

SIG SIG = Sylloge Inscriptionum Graecarum3; superscript omitted in text—List 3

B B = Barnabas (the Letter of), II a.d., except in series of uncial witnesses, in which case B refers to Codex Vaticanus (s. also Vat.). When the abbrv. B would ambiguous, Vat. is used for the codex.—List 1

quot. quot. = quotation

  1. fr. = from

relig. relig. = religious

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

GJs GJs = Gospel of James (Protevangelium Jacobi), II a.d.—List 1

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 357
